options.text: Commence oral di/flucloxacillin 25 mg/kg six-hourly for 7 days, then refer to ENT for elective complete sinus excision once the infection has settled
Refer urgently for total parotidectomy under suspicion of an infected first branchial cleft anomaly with facial nerve and parotid duct involvement
Reassure family that the swelling is self-limiting and arrange follow-up review only if the lesion enlarges, discharges, or develops surrounding cellulitis
Express the sinus contents by squeezing the lesion firmly today, then apply topical mupirocin 2% ointment three times daily for 7 days
Commence oral aciclovir 20 mg/kg five times daily for 7 days as empirical treatment for suspected herpes zoster oticus with otalgia
Refer urgently to paediatric ophthalmology for examination under general anaesthesia and consideration of strabismus surgery despite normal Hirschberg and cover-uncover testing today
Reassure parents this is pseudoesotropia from epicanthic folds, document normal Hirschberg and cover-uncover testing, and advise return for review by the routine 4-year preschool vision screen
Patch the left eye with an adhesive eye patch for 2 hours daily for 3 months to prevent amblyopia despite absent strabismus or amblyopia on objective testing today
Commence topical atropine 1% drops once daily to the right eye to penalise the dominant eye and treat presumed amblyopia in the left eye without specialist diagnosis
Order an outpatient MRI brain and orbits under sedation to exclude an intracranial mass causing a sixth cranial nerve palsy despite full painless eye movements documented today
ECT is absolutely contraindicated because of his prior inferior myocardial infarction
ECT is first-line for severe MDD with psychotic features and food refusal
ECT cannot lawfully proceed because he currently lacks capacity to consent
All antidepressants must be ceased before ECT to avoid serotonin syndrome
Cognitive screening is unnecessary because he has no documented dementia diagnosis
Commence olanzapine 5 mg nocte and arrange routine GP follow-up in two weeks
Refer to the local early-psychosis (headspace / EPPIC-aligned) service for assertive multidisciplinary assessment
Diagnose cannabis-induced psychotic disorder and discharge with brief intervention on cessation
Arrange outpatient MRI brain and EEG before any psychiatric treatment is offered
Admit involuntarily under the relevant state Mental Health Act for assessment
Continue daily thiamine 100 mg orally and observe with hourly CIWA-Ar reassessment overnight
Symptom-triggered diazepam 10-20 mg PO 1-2 hourly titrated to CIWA-Ar plus IV Pabrinex
Commence oral carbamazepine 200 mg three times daily as the sole withdrawal agent
Use an intravenous propofol infusion in a ward-based setting with bedside monitoring
Restart small amounts of oral alcohol to taper consumption over 5 days at home
IV high-dose B-complex (Pabrinex 2 pairs IV TDS), correct magnesium, then continue dextrose
Continue dextrose, give a single 100 mg intramuscular dose of thiamine, then monitor
Hold any further IV glucose and commence intravenous magnesium sulfate as monotherapy
Arrange an urgent MRI brain before commencing any pharmacological replacement therapy
Commence oral thiamine 300 mg daily without any parenteral B-vitamin therapy
Add scheduled risperidone 1 mg BD and continue oxycodone, lorazepam, and metoclopramide
Start donepezil 5 mg daily for worsening Alzheimer disease and discharge home
Transfer to closed psychiatric ward under involuntary order for management of psychosis
Minimise deliriogenic drugs, address constipation, optimise non-pharmacological measures, treat UTI only if supported
Commence intravenous ciprofloxacin empirically for presumed urinary tract infection based on urinalysis
Add lithium augmentation to trough 0.4-0.8 mmol/L with renal and thyroid monitoring
Switch venlafaxine to fluoxetine 20 mg daily as monotherapy without any augmentation
Add bupropion 150 mg daily as monoamine combination augmentation strategy
Add diazepam 5 mg BD as anxiolytic augmentation for six weeks duration
Refer urgently for ECT as first-line treatment for resistant depressive illness
Anticholinergic toxidrome from antimuscarinic overdose
Neuroleptic malignant syndrome from antipsychotic dopamine blockade
Serotonin syndrome from triple serotonergic combination
Sympathomimetic toxidrome from stimulant intoxication
Malignant hyperthermia from volatile anaesthetic exposure
Add sodium valproate 500 mg BD as adjunct mood stabiliser
Add quetiapine extended-release 300-600 mg nocte with metabolic monitoring
Switch lithium to carbamazepine 200 mg BD as monotherapy
Stop lithium immediately because of active pregnancy planning
Switch lithium to lamotrigine 200 mg daily as monotherapy
IV sodium bicarbonate 1-2 mmol/kg bolus, narrow QRS to pH 7.50-7.55
IV flumazenil 0.2 mg slow push to reverse benzodiazepine sedation
IV amiodarone 300 mg loading dose for QRS prolongation overnight
Gastric lavage and oral activated charcoal 50 g for decontamination
IV physostigmine 1 mg slow push to reverse anticholinergic effects
Admit involuntarily under the Mental Health Act for inpatient stabilisation
Discharge with a written 'no-suicide contract' signed by the patient
Collaboratively develop a Stanley-Brown safety plan and contact her DBT team
Commence olanzapine 10 mg daily for affective instability and agitation
Refer to drug and alcohol service for substance-use treatment
Watchful waiting for grief resolution over 3-6 months with no treatment
Diagnose prolonged grief disorder and offer structured grief therapy alone
Start a benzodiazepine for short-term anxiolysis and review in 4 weeks
Sertraline 25-50 mg daily plus structured psychotherapy with perinatal-experienced clinician
Recommend immediate next pregnancy as therapeutic to resolve mood symptoms
Discharge home with mother, written safety plan, and outpatient youth mental health referral
Admit (voluntary or involuntary) to adolescent mental health unit and link with LGBTQAI+ youth supports
Commence fluoxetine 10 mg daily tonight in ED and discharge home with parent
Notify child protection services and remove the young person from the home
Reassure as 'normal teenage stress' and arrange routine GP follow-up next week
Salbutamol 12 puffs (100 mcg/puff) via MDI/spacer with ipratropium 4 puffs (21 mcg/puff) via MDI/spacer, plus oral prednisolone 1 mg/kg immediately
Hudson mask oxygen 8 L/min to SpO2 >94%, nebulised salbutamol 5 mg plus ipratropium 250 mcg by O2-driven nebuliser, oral prednisolone 1 mg/kg, repeat 20 min
High-flow nasal prong oxygen at 2 L/kg/min, IV hydrocortisone 4 mg/kg, urgent chest X-ray to exclude pneumonia before any bronchodilator therapy commences now
IV magnesium sulphate 50 mg/kg over 20 min as first-line, oral prednisolone 1 mg/kg, nasal prong O2 at 2 L/min to maintain SpO2 >92%
Nebulised salbutamol 2.5 mg alone via O2-driven nebuliser, oral prednisolone 1 mg/kg, observe one hour, then add ipratropium and consider IV therapy
IV amoxicillin-clavulanate 25 mg/kg 8-hourly for suspected bacterial cervical lymphadenitis with scarlet fever overlap features
High-dose aspirin 30-50 mg/kg/day in four divided doses alone for 7 days, echocardiogram after treatment
IV immunoglobulin 2 g/kg single infusion plus high-dose aspirin 30-50 mg/kg/day, transitioning to low-dose aspirin after defervescence
IV methylprednisolone 30 mg/kg/day for 3 days as first-line to reduce inflammation, reserving IVIG only for non-responders
Observe and discharge home with oral ibuprofen 10 mg/kg 8-hourly as likely benign self-limiting viral exanthem
Refer to paediatric nephrology for renal biopsy to confirm minimal change disease before commencing any therapy
Oral prednisolone 60 mg/m2/day (about 40 mg daily) for 4 weeks, then 40 mg/m2 alternate days for 4 weeks, with low-salt diet
Oral furosemide 1 mg/kg twice daily with strict 800 mL/day fluid restriction to manage the oedema, no corticosteroid therapy
IV 20% albumin 1 g/kg over 4 hours followed by IV furosemide 1 mg/kg, then start oral prednisolone once oedema resolves
Oral enalapril 0.1 mg/kg daily to reduce proteinuria and observe for spontaneous resolution as likely post-infectious glomerulonephritis
Bolus 20 mL/kg 0.9% sodium chloride over 15 minutes, repeat once if shock persists, then rehydrate at 10 mL/kg/hr with 0.45% saline plus potassium
Bolus 10 mL/kg 0.9% sodium chloride over 30 min; calculate deficit (about 4 L) and replace 50% plus maintenance over 24 hr with 0.9% saline + 40 mmol/L KCl
Rapid 40 mL/kg bolus of 0.9% sodium chloride over 1 hour to restore circulating volume, then replace deficit over 24 hours with 0.9% saline
Commence IV insulin infusion at 0.1 units/kg/hour immediately before any fluid bolus, with maintenance fluids of 0.9% sodium chloride plus 5% dextrose
IV 0.9% sodium chloride at maintenance (40 mL/hr) plus 10 mL/kg bolus, with IV sodium bicarbonate 1 mmol/kg to rapidly correct severe acidosis
Oral polyethylene glycol 3350 (Movicol) 2 sachets/kg/day with increased fluids and continued pancreatic enzymes
Oral Gastrografin (diatrizoate meglumine) 50 mL diluted in 200 mL water, with IV fluids if vomiting persists
Nasogastric N-acetylcysteine 10 mL of 20% solution four times daily to break down the inspissated mucus plugs
Lactulose 10 mL three times daily plus a phosphate enema per rectum to stimulate colonic evacuation
Intravenous metoclopramide 0.15 mg/kg 8-hourly with continuous nasogastric suction, nil by mouth 48 hours
Feed breast milk or formula, recheck BGL in 30 min; if still low give buccal 40% dextrose gel
Give an IV bolus of 2 mL/kg of 10% dextrose immediately via secured peripheral cannula access
Observe at the bedside and repeat capillary blood glucose level in 2 hours' time
Commence an IV infusion of 10% dextrose at 60 mL/kg/day via an infusion pump
Administer intramuscular glucagon 0.1 mg/kg as a single dose into the lateral thigh
Commence oral morphine sulphate 0.4 mg/kg/day in divided doses titrated to Finnegan scores
Commence oral phenobarbitone 5 mg/kg/day as monotherapy first-line for neonatal abstinence
Administer a single dose of IV naloxone 0.1 mg/kg via peripheral line
Provide supportive care only (swaddling, low stimulation, small frequent feeds, skin contact)
Commence oral clonidine 1 mcg/kg six-hourly as the first-line pharmacological agent
Slit-lamp examination by ophthalmologist to screen for chronic anterior uveitis
MRI of the right knee with intravenous gadolinium contrast enhancement
Transthoracic echocardiogram to assess for occult pericarditis or valvulitis
Anti-streptolysin O titre to exclude acute post-streptococcal rheumatic fever
Bone marrow aspirate and trephine biopsy to exclude acute leukaemia
0.45% sodium chloride with 5% dextrose and 20 mmol/L potassium chloride at maintenance rate
Hartmann's (Ringer's lactate) solution at 1.5 times maintenance rate via peripheral IV cannula
0.9% sodium chloride 20 mL/kg boluses until perfusion improves, then 0.9% saline plus 5% dextrose
0.9% sodium chloride with 5% dextrose and 40 mmol/L potassium chloride at maintenance rate
5% dextrose with 0.2% sodium chloride at twice maintenance rate via peripheral IV cannula
Administer intravenous immunoglobulin 2 g/kg to clear residual circulating maternal anti-Ro autoantibodies
Monitor closely for low cardiac output and refer cardiology for pacemaker if bradycardia persists
Start oral prednisolone 2 mg/kg/day to suppress autoimmune-mediated injury to the conduction system
Observe without monitoring as the heart block typically resolves spontaneously within first 6 months
Insert a temporary transvenous pacemaker immediately to increase the ventricular rate to 100 bpm
Commence empirical oral trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole and arrange outpatient renal ultrasound scan after recovery
Admit for IV ampicillin and gentamicin after cultures, with inpatient renal ultrasound during admission
Obtain a catheter urine specimen to confirm the diagnosis before commencing empirical antibiotics
Treat as presumed viral illness with regular antipyretics and clinical review in 24 hours
Perform a lumbar puncture for cerebrospinal fluid analysis before commencing any empirical antibiotics
Non-surgical reduction via air enema under fluoroscopic guidance
Emergency laparotomy with open manual reduction of the intussusception
Observation with serial abdominal examinations and analgesia
Diagnostic laparoscopy with laparoscopic reduction of the intussusception
Intravenous broad-spectrum antibiotics with bowel rest and nil orally
Oral polyethylene glycol 3350 (Movicol) 2 sachets/kg/day with increased fluids and ambulation
Oral Gastrografin (diatrizoate meglumine) 50-100 mL in water, IV fluids if vomiting persists
Nasogastric N-acetylcysteine 10 mL of 20% solution four times daily as sole therapy
Lactulose 10 mL three times daily plus a phosphate enema per rectum overnight
Intravenous metoclopramide 0.15 mg/kg 8-hourly and nil by mouth for 48 hours
Commence IV N-acetylcysteine via the three-bag protocol (150, 50, 100 mg/kg) and serially monitor liver function tests with INR
Activate oral charcoal 50 g as ingestion was within 2 hours, then reassess the need for N-acetylcysteine therapy
Observe without active treatment as ALT and INR are currently normal, then repeat bloods in 12 hours for delayed toxicity
Refer urgently for liver transplantation evaluation as the high paracetamol level and symptoms indicate irreversible hepatic necrosis
Administer IV pantoprazole 40 mg for gastric protection and arrange psychiatric review for the intentional overdose
Commence TNF inhibitor infliximab 5 mg/kg IV at 0, 2, and 6 weeks, then 8-weekly, with continued immunomodulator
Increase azathioprine dose to 3 mg/kg/day and continue mesalazine, expecting the fistula to heal with medical therapy alone
Perform a fistulotomy with primary closure under general anaesthesia and continue current azathioprine
Insert a Seton drain and treat with oral metronidazole 10 mg/kg three times daily for 6 months
Refer for diverting colostomy as perianal Crohn's disease is refractory to all medical therapy
Intravenous midazolam infusion 0.1-0.2 mg/kg/hr titrated to seizure suppression, escalate to thiopentone if ineffective
Intravenous phenytoin 20 mg/kg loading dose over 20 minutes with cardiac monitoring
Intravenous propofol 2 mg/kg bolus followed by continuous infusion in ICU
Intramuscular paraldehyde 0.4 mL/kg into the lateral thigh single dose
Intravenous magnesium sulphate 50 mg/kg loading dose over 20 minutes via pump
IV mannitol 0.5-1 g/kg over 20 min or hypertonic saline 3% 5 mL/kg, then urgent CT brain
Stop insulin infusion, administer 2 mL/kg of 50% dextrose IV bolus, then hydrocortisone 50 mg IV
Intubate for airway protection and hyperventilate to pCO2 of 25-30 mmHg to reduce intracranial pressure
Increase the rate of normal saline infusion to 20 mL/kg/hr to improve cerebral perfusion pressure
Reduce the IV insulin infusion to 0.05 units/kg/hr and continue close monitoring for clinical improvement
Superior mesenteric artery (SMA) syndrome
Chronic partial duodenal atresia
Congenital annular pancreas obstruction
Eosinophilic oesophagitis with stricture
Coeliac artery compression syndrome
Administer IV 3% hypertonic saline 5 mL/kg over 20 minutes and elevate head midline by 30 degrees
Give intravenous mannitol 1 g/kg single dose over 20 minutes via secure peripheral IV cannula
Immediately hyperventilate to a pCO2 of 25-30 mmHg to reduce cerebral blood volume and pressure
Insert an intracranial pressure monitor and induce thiopentone coma in the paediatric intensive care unit
Administer intravenous dexamethasone 0.5 mg/kg as a single dose to reduce vasogenic cerebral oedema
Switch to adalimumab 160 mg then 80 mg week 2, then 40 mg fortnightly, plus methotrexate
Increase the infliximab dose to 10 mg/kg every 4 weeks and continue current azathioprine therapy
Switch to ustekinumab (anti-IL-12/23 agent) as first-line alternative to anti-TNF biologic therapy
Add oral prednisolone 40 mg daily to suppress the flare and continue current infliximab regimen
Stop all biologic therapy and refer the patient for elective subtotal colectomy with ileostomy
IV methylprednisolone 1 g daily 5 days plus IVIG 2 g/kg over 5 days, urgent laparoscopic oophorectomy
Start oral risperidone 2 mg twice daily for psychosis and schedule routine outpatient psychiatric review next week
Initiate IV aciclovir 10 mg/kg 8-hourly for presumed herpes simplex encephalitis until CSF PCR returns
Perform therapeutic plasma exchange alone as sole immunomodulatory therapy, without corticosteroids, to avoid psychiatric worsening
Administer intravenous rituximab 375 mg/m2 weekly for 4 weeks as the first-line immunomodulatory therapy
Wilson disease with acute liver failure and Coombs-negative haemolysis; check ceruloplasmin and 24-hour urinary copper
Autoimmune hepatitis; test for ANA, anti-smooth muscle antibody, and liver-kidney microsomal type 1 antibody
Acute paracetamol toxicity; send serum paracetamol level and commence N-acetylcysteine without delay
Hepatitis A infection; test serum hepatitis A IgM antibodies and arrange supportive care
Budd-Chiari syndrome; arrange Doppler ultrasound of the hepatic veins for confirmation
Rapid splitting of the treating team into all-good and all-bad
Multiple low-lethality self-harm episodes since early adolescence onward
Two recent emergency presentations following heated relationship arguments
Chronic subjective feelings of emptiness lasting many years now
Tearful affect that brightens reactively within the clinical interview
Rigid preoccupation with order and control
Hoarding of work documents at home
Reluctance to delegate tasks to others
Long working hours with high productivity
Behaviours felt as appropriate, not intrusive
Risperidone 0.25 mg BD reviewed at 12 weeks for ongoing clinical need
Olanzapine 10 mg nocte continued indefinitely without scheduled review
Haloperidol 2 mg BD for ongoing delusional content and agitation
Quetiapine 100 mg BD as first-line treatment for psychosis
Lorazepam 1 mg TDS scheduled regularly for ongoing agitation
Restrict cow milk and commence oral iron 3 mg/kg/day, recheck in 4 weeks
Refer for inpatient packed red cell transfusion given Hb less than 90 g/L
Order haemoglobin electrophoresis and HbA2 quantification before commencing any treatment
Day-stay IV iron polymaltose infusion now in the haematology day unit
Multivitamin containing 5 mg elemental iron daily, continue current milk intake
Commence oral levothyroxine 100 microg daily and recheck thyroid function tests in 6-8 weeks
Commence carbimazole 15 mg daily plus propranolol 20 mg three times daily orally
Arrange thyroid ultrasound and fine-needle aspiration of the goitre before any treatment
Defer treatment for 3 months to allow for possible spontaneous resolution of subclinical disease
Commence oral levothyroxine 50 microg daily (about 1.2-1.7 microg/kg) and recheck TFTs in 6-8 weeks
Primary herpes simplex gingivostomatitis with secondary autoinoculation to the hands
Varicella with mucosal involvement and acral spread from secondary scratching
Hand-foot-and-mouth disease due to enterovirus, most often coxsackievirus A16 subtype
Herpangina due to coxsackievirus with secondary autoinoculation to the hands
Scabies with secondary bacterial impetiginisation of the wrists and feet
Remove central venous line immediately and culture catheter tip before any antibiotics
Paired cultures plus IV meropenem and vancomycin with 20 mL/kg saline bolus, within 60 minutes
Urgent CT abdomen and pelvis to exclude typhlitis before commencing any antibiotics
IV piperacillin-tazobactam alone, then reassess at 30 minutes before any escalation
Give granulocyte colony-stimulating factor immediately to raise the neutrophil count first
Transfuse one paediatric unit of packed red cells as treatment
Refer urgently for bone marrow aspirate to exclude underlying leukaemia
Haemoglobin electrophoresis and faecal occult blood testing first-line before iron
Oral elemental iron, reduce cow's milk below 500 mL, recheck FBC at 4 weeks
Arrange outpatient IV iron polymaltose infusion as first-line treatment now
